quiz.question:
Black has gone into c3, and they're attacking the b3-pawn. The king also helps support the d-pawn. 

What's the best plan for White?

answers.text: Going for a draw with dynamic the dynamic c5-pawn push!

feedback.text:
c5 is the only move to draw. 

It mounts a formidable defense against the passed d-pawn. If Black ignores it, you can play c6! 

If Black takes it, you can recapture with the rook and disturb the king's support of the d-pawn.

distractors.text: Winning with f5, going for the glory with the f-pawn.

feedback.text: The d-pawn is much faster than the f-pawn. Black could promote and win easily.

distractors.text: Defending / going for a draw with Kf3.

feedback.text: The pawn is too quick. White cannot defend against it. If Kf3, ...d2. If Ke2, Re1+!
